content: The open-ended exploratory drive that differentiates intrinsic curiosity methods like ICM and RND from conventional curious RL agents can be reframed as a graded 'epistemic maturity' trait for the agent itself, where the type of curiosity mechanism an agent uses directly shapes its developmental trajectory in sparse-reward open-ended environments. This trait mirrors how human agents' inherent levels of information-seeking openness shape the long-term complexity of their memories and real-world learning outcomes.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
